title: CSE 234 PA1 - Automatic Differentiation Framework
summary: Implementation-order module DAG for the PA1 autodiff framework task, including graph-construction factories, core tensor ops, fused ops, and transformer training scaffolding.
instructionPreview: This is the first programming assignment for a machine-learning systems course. You will implement a complete automatic differentiation (autodiff) framework from scratch, including all arithmetic and tensor operations, graph evaluation, reverse-mode differentiation, and fused compound operations. The framework uses a computational-graph style (similar to TensorFlow v1) where you first build a graph of `Node` objects, then evaluate it or differentiate it.
instruction:
This is the first programming assignment for a machine-learning systems course. You will implement a complete automatic differentiation (autodiff) framework from scratch, including all arithmetic and tensor operations, graph evaluation, reverse-mode differentiation, and fused compound operations. The framework uses a computational-graph style (similar to TensorFlow v1) where you first build a graph of `Node` objects, then evaluate it or differentiate it.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is in `/workspace/pa1/`. You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

The following modules need to be implemented or substantially built out:

- **Graph Node Construction** (`pa1/auto_diff.py`): Restore the graph-building API itself: `Variable`, `placeholder`, and the `__call__` builders behind every global op instance (`add`, `mul`, `sum_op`, `broadcast`, `matmul`, `softmax`, `layernorm`, `relu`, `sqrt`, `power`, `mean`, and related helpers) must create `Node` objects with the correct inputs, attrs, and debug names before any forward/backward math can run.

- **Core Autodiff Math & Execution** (`pa1/auto_diff.py`): Implement the forward compute and backward gradient methods for the arithmetic, comparison, reduction, broadcast, linear algebra, normalization, and activation ops (addition, multiplication, division, subtraction, comparison, matmul, transpose, softmax, layer normalization, ReLU, sqrt, power, mean, sum, broadcast, expand, log, zeros-like, ones-like), the Node operator overloads (`__add__`, `__sub__`, `__rsub__`, `__mul__`, `__truediv__`), the graph topological sort, the graph evaluator, and the reverse-mode gradient computation.

- **Fused Operations** (`pa1/fused_ops.py`): Implement both fused node builders and fused compound operations that combine matrix multiplication with either layer normalization or softmax into single graph nodes, including forward computation and backward gradient propagation.

- **Transformer Forward Graph** (`pa1/transformer.py`): Build the `transformer()` graph for a single-head attention encoder block, including Q/K/V projections, scaled dot-product attention, output projection, layer normalization, feed-forward layers with broadcasted biases, and sequence-wise pooling for classification. (~1 file, ~1 function to implement)

- **Training Utilities** (`pa1/transformer.py`): Utility functions that sit on top of the autodiff engine: `softmax_loss()` for average cross-entropy over one-hot labels, and `sgd_epoch()` for the scaffolded fixed-size mini-batch SGD loop that consumes evaluator outputs, reduces any leading batch dimension in returned gradients, skips incomplete tail batches, updates model weights, and reports epoch loss.

- **Training Driver Scaffold** (`pa1/transformer.py`): Finish `train_model()` by instantiating the transformer parameter placeholders, wiring the forward graph and gradient/evaluator construction, filling the `f_run_model()` / `f_eval_model()` tensor mappings, and initializing `model_weights` in the scaffold's expected parameter order. The scaffold is intentionally split so the graph-building half and the runtime binding half are both tested.

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite. You must implement them with these exact signatures:

The tests also exercise the graph-construction callables themselves: `Variable(...)`, `placeholder(...)`, the global autodiff op instances such as `add(...)`, `sum_op(...)`, `broadcast(...)`, `matmul(...)`, `softmax(...)`, `layernorm(...)`, and the fused builders `matmul_layernorm(...)` / `matmul_softmax(...)`. These APIs must create `Node` objects with the correct inputs, attrs, and names.

In `pa1/auto_diff.py`:
- `Node.__add__(self, other)` — dispatches to element-wise add (Node) or add-by-constant (scalar)
- `Node.__sub__(self, other)` — element-wise subtraction
- `Node.__rsub__(self, other)` — reverse subtraction
- `Node.__mul__(self, other)` — dispatches to element-wise multiply (Node) or multiply-by-constant (scalar)
- `Node.__truediv__(self, other)` — dispatches to element-wise divide (Node) or divide-by-constant (scalar)
- `AddOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — element-wise addition of two tensors
- `AddOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — partial adjoints for addition
- `AddByConstOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — add tensor and scalar constant
- `AddByConstOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient through constant addition
- `MulOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — element-wise multiplication of two tensors
- `MulOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — partial adjoints for multiplication
- `MulByConstOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — multiply tensor by scalar constant
- `MulByConstOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient through constant multiplication
- `SubOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — element-wise subtraction
- `SubOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — partial adjoints for subtraction
- `GreaterThanOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — element-wise comparison as float tensor
- `GreaterThanOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of comparison (zero)
- `ZerosLikeOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — all-zero tensor matching input shape
- `ZerosLikeOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient through zeros-like
- `OnesLikeOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — all-one tensor matching input shape
- `OnesLikeOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient through ones-like
- `SumOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — sum along dimensions in `node.attrs["dim"]`
- `SumOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of sum reduction
- `ExpandAsOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — broadcast tensor to shape of another
- `ExpandAsOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of broadcast
- `ExpandAsOp3d.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — 3D broadcast with unsqueeze
- `ExpandAsOp3d.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of 3D broadcast
- `LogOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — natural logarithm
- `LogOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of logarithm
- `BroadcastOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — broadcast to `node.attrs["target_shape"]`
- `BroadcastOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of broadcast with dimension reduction
- `DivOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — element-wise division
- `DivOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — partial adjoints for division
- `DivByConstOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — divide tensor by scalar constant
- `DivByConstOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient through constant division
- `TransposeOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — swap dimensions `node.attrs["dim0"]` and `node.attrs["dim1"]`
- `TransposeOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of transpose
- `MatMulOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — batched matrix multiplication
- `MatMulOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — partial adjoints for matmul
- `SoftmaxOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — softmax along `node.attrs["dim"]`
- `SoftmaxOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — Jacobian-vector product for softmax
- `LayerNormOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — layer normalization with `node.attrs["normalized_shape"]` and `node.attrs["eps"]`
- `LayerNormOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of layer norm
- `ReLUOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — element-wise ReLU activation
- `ReLUOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of ReLU
- `SqrtOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — element-wise square root
- `SqrtOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of sqrt
- `PowerOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — element-wise power with `node.attrs["exponent"]`
- `PowerOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of power
- `MeanOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — mean reduction over `node.attrs["dim"]` with `node.attrs["keepdim"]`
- `MeanOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — gradient of mean
- `topological_sort(nodes)` — returns all nodes reachable from `nodes` in topological order (inputs before dependents)
- `Evaluator.run(self, input_values: Dict[Node, torch.Tensor]) -> List[torch.Tensor]` — evaluates all nodes in `self.eval_nodes` by traversing the graph in topological order, using `input_values` for placeholder nodes
- `gradients(output_node: Node, nodes: List[Node]) -> List[Node]` — constructs the backward computational graph; returns one gradient node per requested input node via reverse-mode autodiff

In `pa1/fused_ops.py`:
- `MatMulLayerNormOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — fused matmul + layer norm forward
- `MatMulLayerNormOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — chain rule through LayerNorm then MatMul; return `[grad_A, grad_B]` as graph nodes
- `MatMulSoftmaxOp.compute(self, node, input_values) -> torch.Tensor` — fused matmul + softmax forward
- `MatMulSoftmaxOp.gradient(self, node, output_grad) -> List[Node]` — chain rule through softmax then MatMul; return `[grad_A, grad_B]` as graph nodes

In `pa1/transformer.py`:
- `transformer(X: Node, nodes: List[Node], model_dim: int, seq_length: int, eps, batch_size, num_classes) -> Node` — build the single-head attention encoder graph with broadcasted biases and sequence-wise classification pooling
- `softmax_loss(Z: Node, y_one_hot: Node, batch_size: int) -> Node` — average softmax cross-entropy over one-hot labels using the autodiff ops from `pa1/auto_diff.py`
- `sgd_epoch(f_run_model: Callable, X: torch.Tensor, y: torch.Tensor, model_weights: List[torch.Tensor], batch_size: int, lr: float)` — run one epoch of the scaffolded fixed-size mini-batch SGD loop, calling `f_run_model(X_batch, y_batch, model_weights)`, reducing returned gradients to parameter shape, updating weights, and returning the average epoch loss
- `train_model() -> float` — build the transformer training graph, initialize the parameter list in `[W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, W_1, W_2, b_1, b_2]` order, and return the final test accuracy from the provided MNIST scaffold

All gradient methods must return lists of `Node` objects (not tensors) — they build the backward graph using the existing ops. The `gradients()` function similarly returns a list of `Node` objects representing the gradient graph.

moduleDag.description: Implementation-order module DAG for the PA1 autodiff framework task, including graph-construction factories, core tensor ops, fused ops, and transformer training scaffolding.

nodes.label: Graph Node Factories
nodes.description: Restore Variable construction plus the __call__ builders for placeholder, arithmetic, reduction, broadcast, linear algebra, normalization, and activation ops in auto_diff.py so every global op instance creates Node objects with correct inputs, attrs, and debug names.

nodes.label: Fused Node Factories
nodes.description: Restore the fused MatMul+LayerNorm and MatMul+Softmax __call__ builders so fused_ops exposes graph nodes with the right attrs and names before any fused forward/backward math is implemented.

nodes.label: Node Dispatch Ops
nodes.description: Restore the Node operator overloads so Python arithmetic on Node instances dispatches to the correct global op builders or constant-specialized variants.

nodes.label: Elementwise Arithmetic Ops
nodes.description: Restore Add/AddByConst/Mul/MulByConst compute and gradient rules so the foundational scalar and elementwise autodiff primitives evaluate and propagate adjoints correctly.

nodes.label: Comparison, Mask, And Division Utils
nodes.description: Restore the comparison, subtraction, zeros/ones-like, and division primitives so masking, constant baselines, and quotient gradients all work in the autodiff graph.

nodes.label: Reduction And Expand Ops
nodes.description: Restore sum reduction plus the expand_as helpers so reduction outputs and broadcasted gradient reshaping work for both 2D and 3D tensor shapes.

nodes.label: Broadcast And Log Ops
nodes.description: Restore the explicit broadcast and logarithm ops so scalar-to-tensor expansion and log-based loss building both work with correct backward reductions.

nodes.label: Linear Algebra Ops
nodes.description: Restore transpose and batched matmul so matrix reordering and attention-style tensor products work in both forward evaluation and gradient graph construction.

nodes.label: Activation And Scalar Math Ops
nodes.description: Restore ReLU, sqrt, power, and mean so nonlinearities and scalar reductions used by normalization and transformer heads evaluate and backpropagate correctly.

nodes.label: Topological Sort And Evaluator
nodes.description: Restore the forward graph execution utilities so node lists are topologically ordered and Evaluator.run can execute multi-output graphs with placeholder validation.

nodes.label: Reverse-Mode Gradients
nodes.description: Restore gradients() so reverse-mode autodiff seeds the output with ones_like, accumulates adjoints along the reversed topological order, and returns zero-like grads for disconnected nodes.

nodes.label: Softmax Ops
nodes.description: Restore softmax forward and Jacobian-vector-product logic so probability normalization works inside losses, attention, and fused softmax backprop.

nodes.label: LayerNorm Ops
nodes.description: Restore layer normalization forward and backward logic so normalized hidden states match torch.nn.functional.layer_norm and backpropagate through the reconstructed statistics.

nodes.label: Fused MatMul LayerNorm Op
nodes.description: Restore the fused matmul+layernorm forward and backward rules so the fused node matches the separate matmul->layernorm graph in both outputs and gradients.

nodes.label: Fused MatMul Softmax Op
nodes.description: Restore the fused matmul+softmax forward and backward rules so the fused node behaves identically to the separate matmul->softmax graph.

nodes.label: Transformer Forward Graph
nodes.description: Restore the single-head attention encoder graph so transformer() builds attention, layernorm, feed-forward, broadcasted bias, and pooled classifier nodes using the autodiff APIs.

nodes.label: Softmax Loss Graph
nodes.description: Restore softmax_loss() so the average cross-entropy graph is built from the autodiff softmax, log, and reduction primitives.

nodes.label: SGD Epoch Loop
nodes.description: Restore sgd_epoch() so the scaffolded batch loop slices inputs, skips incomplete tail batches, reduces gradient batch axes, updates weights, and returns average epoch loss.

nodes.label: Train Model Graph Build
nodes.description: Restore the graph-building half of train_model() so the parameter placeholders, transformer forward graph, loss node, gradient node list, and evaluator construction all follow the scaffolded training design.

nodes.label: Train Model Runtime Bindings
nodes.description: Restore the runtime half of train_model() so the evaluator input mappings and initial model_weights list line up with the scaffolded parameter order used by the training loop and eval loop.

edges.label: Fused op builders still rely on the core Node/Variable/Placeholder graph-construction API from auto_diff.
Node arithmetic overloads dispatch into the global op instances whose __call__ builders must already create graph nodes.
The add/mul/etc. compute and gradient tests call the global op instances, so their __call__ builders must already work.
Subtraction and division gradients reuse mul/add/div primitives from the basic arithmetic block.
Expand and reduction gradient helpers rely on zeros/ones-like, subtraction, and division-style base behavior.
Broadcast gradients reuse reduction/expand helpers to collapse broadcasted axes back to the input shape.
The matmul/transpose block is implemented on top of already-working reduction and expansion helpers used later in its gradients.
ReLU/sqrt/power/mean gradients depend on comparison, multiplication, and division-style primitives from the base block.
Mean gradients need sum/expand helpers to re-broadcast the upstream reduction result.
Evaluator tests use add/mul graphs, so those primitive compute paths need to work before forward graph execution can be validated.
The reverse-mode engine builds on topological traversal and Evaluator-based execution of the gradient graph.
Softmax gradients reuse log/reduction-style helper ops and explicit expansion of the dot-product term.
Softmax is exercised later in attention/fused paths that already assume the linear algebra ops exist.
LayerNorm gradients reconstruct means/variances with reductions and expand the statistics back to the input shape.
LayerNorm gradients depend on mean, sqrt, power, division, and subtraction helpers from the scalar math stack.
The fused matmul+layernorm implementation needs the fused node builder before its forward/backward math can be validated.
The fused layernorm gradient propagates back through matmul via transpose and batched matmul.
The fused layernorm gradient mirrors the standalone LayerNorm backward logic.
The fused matmul+softmax implementation needs the fused node builder before its forward/backward math can be validated.
The fused softmax gradient backpropagates through matmul using transpose and batched matmul.
The fused softmax gradient mirrors the standalone Softmax Jacobian-vector product.
transformer() composes matmul, transpose, and explicit bias broadcasting to build the attention and projection graph.
transformer() uses softmax for attention weights in the scaled dot-product block.
transformer() uses layer normalization in both the attention and feed-forward subgraphs.
softmax_loss() combines softmax with log and reduction nodes from the autodiff library.
softmax_loss() depends on the standalone softmax op for its probability normalization.
train_model() first needs working Variable construction to create the parameter placeholders and input nodes.
train_model() wires the transformer forward graph into the training scaffold.
train_model() builds the objective from softmax_loss() before constructing gradients and evaluators.
train_model() uses ad.gradients() to create the backward graph for the model parameters.
train_model() instantiates Evaluator objects once the forward/backward graphs are built.
The SGD loop is validated against the loss/gradient contract that the training graph returns.
The runtime closures and weight list are the final step after the placeholders, forward graph, and evaluators already exist.
train_model() hands its closures and initial weights into sgd_epoch(), so the runtime bindings must match the SGD loop contract.
